LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE TO DEFENDANT DUNCAN EASTWOOD RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE LEGAL REMEDY IS INADEQUATE. THE SUIT INVOLVES MONETARY DAMAGES THAT ARE READILY ASCERTAINABLE AND WOULD ADEQUATELY COMPENSATE PLAINTIFF IF HE PREVAILS. INADEQUATE SHOWING OF IRREPARABLE, IMMINENT HARM ABSENT THE INJUNCTION. NO DETAILED FINANCIAL INFORMATION PROVIDED TO SUPPORT ALLEGATIONS. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT DEMONSTRATE A HIGH PROBABILITY OF SUCCESS AT TRIAL, IN LIGHT OF THE OPPOSING EVIDENCE AND ALLEGATIONS. IT IS NOT CLEAR THAT BALANCE OF EQUITIES FAVOR PLAINTIFF, AND BOTH PARTIES ALLEGE IMPROPER CONDUCT ON THE PART OF THE OTHER. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF AND CROSS DEFENDANT JOSEPH BANDIERA DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION, NO OPPOSITION. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION. CROSS-COMPLAINANT TO PLEAD DAMAGES AS A RESULT OF THE HARM, SPECIFYING DAMAGES ON THE FIDUCIARY DUTY CLAIM, AND THAT CROSS-DEFENDANT TOOK PROPERTY THAT WAS RIGHTLY CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S ON THE CONVERSION CLAIM. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION. (MR. MAYO WILL PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR # 5797

Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause To Defendant Duncan Eastwood Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Mar-16-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff's Order to Show Cause to Defendant Duncan Eastwood Re: Preliminary Injunction. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for preliminary injunction is denied. Plaintiff fails to show that the legal remedy is inadequate. The suit involves monetary damages that are readily ascertainable and would adequately compensate plaintiff if he prevails. Inadequate showing of irreparable, imminent harm absent the injunction. No detailed financial information provided to support allegations. Plaintiff does not demonstrate a high probability of success at trial, in light of the opposing evidence and allegations. It is not clear that balance of equities favor plaintiff, and both parties allege improper conduct on the part of the other. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-08-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF AND CROSS DEFENDANT JOSEPH BANDIERA DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Appearances: Plaintiff/Cross Defendant Joseph Bandiera appearing in Pro Per and moving party; Terence Mayo, Esq. of Mayo & Mayo (415) 397-1515 appearing for Defendant and cross-complainant Duncan Eastwood. (The tentative ruling is not being contested.) The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to first and second causes of action, no opposition. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to third and fourth causes of action. Cross-complainant to plead damages as a result of the harm, specifying damages on the fiduciary duty claim, and that cross-defendant took property that was rightly cross-complainant's on the conversion claim. Demurrer overruled as to the fifth cause of action. (Mr. Mayo will prepare proposed Order.)(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR # 5797
